Chaotic Systems
The Three-Body Problem can be compared to chaotic systems, where small changes can lead to unpredictable and complex outcomes. Just as a butterfly flapping its wings can cause a hurricane on the other side of the world, the three suns in the Three-Body Problem create a chaotic system where small changes can have significant effects.
